question: TMC is an acronym of
explanation: TMC stands for Thousand Million Cubic Feet. It is commonly used in India in reference to volume of water in a reservoir or river flow. 109 1 TMC ft is equivalent to cubic feet. question: Which of the following devices is used to forecast weather?
explanation: The common instruments for measure weather forecasting are Anemometer, Wind Vane, Hygrometer, Thermometer etc. • Hygrometer is used to measure the humidity or the water vapour in the air. • Odometer is used to measure the distance travelled by vehicle. • Fluxmeter is used to measure magnetic flux of a permanent magnet. • A refractometer is a laboratory or field device for the measurement of an index of refraction.

explanation: An Anemometer is a device used for measuring wind speed and direction. It is also a common weather station instrument. Type of Anemometers are – • Cup Anemometer • Hot-wire Anemometer • Wind mill Anemometer • Vane Anemometer • Aerometer – An aerometer is an instrument designed to measure the density of air and some other gases. • Spectrometer– For a visible light a spectrometer can separate with light and measure individual narrow band of colors, called spectrum. • Speedometer– It is a gauge that measure and displays the instantaneous speed of vehicle.

question: ______ is an instrument for measuring a small electric current.

Sphygmomanometer :- An instrument for measuring blood pressure. Hygrometer :- It is an instrument used to measure the amount of water in air, in soil or in confined spaces. Anemometer :- It is a device used for measuring wind speed and direction.

explanation: An Anemometer is an instrument that measures wind speed and wind pressure. explanation: A barometer is a scientific instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure, also called barometric pressure. question: Match List-I and List-II correctly and select your answer using the codes given below: List-I List-II A. Seismograph 1. instrument for recording thunder and lightning B. question: A passenger sitting inside a stationary bus is thrown back when the driver suddenly starts the bus due to
explanation: When a bus starts suddenly the passenger tend to jerk backward. This is because their body possess inertia due to which the passengers tend to remain in their state of rest even when the bus has started moving.

question: A body having very high mass is bound to have high _________.
explanation: A body that has a very high mass is bound to have high inertia. The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion varies with mass. Mass is that quantity that is solely dependent upon the inertia of an object.

question: When milk is churned cream gets separated due to

question: Which of the following is the principle cause for a passenger of bend forward when the driver of a bus applies brakes to stop the bus?
explanation: The passengers in a bus bend forward when driver applies breaks to stop the bus because of their inertia as they are in state of motion even when the bus has came to rest. Science

question: Identify the correct option that explains law of inertia.
explanation: Newton's first law of motion explains about inertia affects moving and non moving object. Newton's first law states that an object will remain at rest or move at a constant speed in a straight line unless it is acted on by an external force. Inertia comes from mass objects with more mass have more inertia. Newton's second law of motion:- "The rate of change of momentum of a body is directly proportional to the applied force and takes place in the direction in which force acts". Newton's third law of motion:- "Whenever one object exerts a force on a second object, the second object exerts equal and opposite force on the first object."

question: ------------ is the ability of a quantum system to be in multiple states at the same time until it is measured.
explanation: Superposition is the ability of a quantum system to be in multiple states at the same time until it is measured. It is one of the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics.

question: “An object at rest will remain at rest and an object moving at a constant velocity will continue moving at a constant velocity, unless it is acted on by an unbalanced force”. This law is called as––– 488 / 896
explanation: Newton’s first law of motion states that a body at rest remains at rest or a body in motion remains in motion at the same velocity unless acted upon by an external force. This law is also known as Law of Inertia. There are three laws of motion were first stated by Isaac Newton in 1687. Newton's 2nd Law of Motion– A body acted upon by a force moves in such a manner that the time rate of change of momentum equals the force. 3rd Newton's Law of Motion– If two bodies exerts a forces on each other, these forces are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction. Sometimes it referred as "Action-Reaction Law".

question: Which of the following phenomenon is responsible for Coriolis force?

options.b: Revolution of earth around the sun
options.d: Rotation of the earth around its axis

explanation: Rotation of the earth around its axis is due to effect of Coriolis force. Because of the earth rotates on its axis, circulating air is deflected toward the right in the Northern Hemisphere and toward left in Southern Hemisphere. This deflection is called the Coriolis Effect. (cid:1) The Coriolis impact is responsible for many massive- scale weather patterns.

question: The principle of dialysis is based on
explanation: Dialysis is a process where a person with nonfunctioning kidneys can have their blood filtered by a machine. • Dialysis works on the principle of the diffusion of solutes and ultra filtration of fluid across a semi- permeable membrane. Diffusion is a property of substances in water, tend to move from an area of high concentration to an area of low concentration. Dialysis is the process of removing excess water, solutes and toxins from the blood in the people whose kidneys can no longer perform these functions naturally. The first successful dialysis was performed in 1943.

question: The flying of birds is a proof of Newton's

options.d: Both second and third law of motion

explanation: The flying of a bird is based on action and reaction force that is Newton's third law. While flying, the bird pushes the air down with its wings to get an equal and opposite reaction in the upward direction, which helps the bird in going up. Science

question: The weight of a body at the centre of Earth will be :

options.a: The same at that on the surface of the earth
options.b: More than that on the surface of the earth
options.c: About half of that on the surface of the earth

explanation: The weight of a body placed of the centre of the Earth is zero. This is because the mass at the exact centre of the Earth is zero. Gravitational force exists only if there is mass in the object.

question: An object weight maximum in-
explanation: There is no buoyant force in a vacuum so the weight of the body is maximum in the vacuum. • The buoyant force is also called up thrust. It is equal to the product of the mass of displaced liquid or gas and gravitational acceleration.

question: The gravitational force with which the Sun attracts the Earth-

options.a: Is less than the force with which the Earth attracts the Sun
options.b: Is the same as the force with which Earth attracts the Sun
options.c: Is more than the force with which the Earth attracts the Sun

This is equivalent to 3.5 kJ/s (3.5 kW) or 210 kJ/min. 1 TR = 12660 kJ/h = 210 kJ/min = 3.5 kW 1 TR = 50 kcal/min One ton of refrigeration corresponds to 50 kcal/min Science

question: Light 169. Which one of the following rays carry 50 percent of the total energy of the Solar Spectrum?
explanation: Solar radiation is radiant energy from the sun. It provides light and heat for the Earth and energy for photosynthesis. The three relevant bands, or ranges, along the solar radiation spectrum are ultraviolet visible and infrared. Infrared radiation makes up 49.4%.

question: Which colour is seen in middle of the rainbow?
explanation: The 7 colour bands of a rainbow are violet, Indigo, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange and Red (VIBGYOR). So, Green is the middle colour with 3 colours (Violet, Indigo & Blue) on one side and the remaining three on other side.

question: Why is red light used to stop traffic in traffic signals?

options.c: It is visible to people even having bad eyesight

explanation: As the wavelength of red light is maximum so it is least scattered by the atmospheric particles therefore it is used in traffic signals.

question: A car parked in the sun with its windows closed gets terribly hot inside. This is due to

options.a: The head emitted by the engine
options.b: The good conducting power of the metal body

explanation: The inside of a car with its windows closed is hotter than outside due to green house effect as the glass in the windows absorb infrared radiations of the Sun but do not allow them to escape or reflect back, thus increasing the temperature of the car on the inside.

question: As an object approaches the speed of light, it's mass becomes
explanation: When an object approaches the speed of light its mass becomes infinite and so does the energy required to move it. That means it is impossible for any matter to go faster than light travels, • If an object tries to travel 186,000 miles/second, its mass become infinite. • According to theory of relativity if v (velocity of the object) becomes equal to the c (speed of light) then, m 0 m= v2 1− c2 498 / 896 ∵v = c m m= 0 c2 1− c2 m=∞ Mass of the object (m) will be infinite.

question: Which of the following has the highest energy?
explanation: Blue light has the highest energy and has a wavelength of approximately 460-500 (nm). • We know that in spectrum violet has minimum wavelength so maximum energy. • The lowest frequency of visible light which is red has the least energy.

question: What colour of light is formed when red, blue and green colours of light meet in equal proportion?
explanation: The three primary colours in additive mixing are red, green and blue. In the absence of light of any colour, the result is black. If all three primary colors of light are mixed in equal proportions, the result is makes white. When the red and green lights mix, the result is yellow, when green and blue lights mix, the result is blue when the blue and red lights mix, the results is magenta. • White light is a combination of all colours in the colour spectrum.

question: Scattering of a beam of light by a medium containing small suspended particles is known as:
explanation: Tyndall effect also known as Tyndall phenomenon is scattering of beam of light by a medium containing small suspended particle e.g. smoke or dust in a room which makes visible, a light beam enterning a window. 19th • The effect is named after the century British physicist John Tyndall.

question: The brilliant red colour of rising and setting sun is due to the ______ of light.
explanation: • As Sunlight travels through the earth’s atmosphere, it get scattered due to atmospheric particle (dust present in the atmosphere). • As per Rayleigh Scattering Theory, the amount of Scattering of light is inversely proportional to the fourth power of the wavelength of light. • Light of shorter wavelength is scattered much more than light of longer wavelengths. Science • Red color have longest wavelength that is why it get scattered least, so the red colour is able to reach our eyes after the atmospheric seattering of light. Therefore, the sun appears reddish early in the morning.

question: What purpose does the ultraviolet (UV) treatment of water serve?
explanation: Ultraviolet water purification is the most effective method for disinfecting bacteria from the water. Ultraviolet (UV) rays penetrate harmful pathogens in home's water and destroy illness-causing microorganisms by attacking their genetic core (DNA). Ultraviolet systems destroy 99.99% of harmful Micro- organism without adding chemicals or changing water’s taste or odor.

question: The formation of rainbow is NOT due to the ______ of light by suspended tiny water droplets.
explanation: Rainbow is formed due to dispersion and total internal reflection. Tiny droplets of water due to very less mass, gets suspended in the atmosphere and acts as a good optical medium. The sunlight, when changes medium, undergoes total internal reflection through these droplets thereby splitting in its components colours and hence results in rainbow formation. • Rainbow is formed during rainy season due to refraction and total internal reflection of rays falling on suspended water droplets.

question: The colours in the fine cut diamond are because of which of the following?

options.a: Variance in the transparency of diamond
options.b: Variance in the index of refraction

explanation: Colours in diamond originate from lattice defects and impurities. The diamond crystal lattice is exceptionally strong and only atoms of nitrogen, boron and hydrogen can be introduced into diamond during the growth at significant concentrations.

question: What is/are the cause(s) of arise hypermetropia?

options.a: Excessive curvature of the eye lens.
options.c: Focal length of the eye lens is too long.

explanation: Hypermetropia is a defect of vision that causes the impossibility for rays of light to be focused on the retina, but behind it. The causes of Hypermetropia are– (i) The sphericity of the eye lens is decreased. (ii) The focal length of the lens increases. (iii) Due to this the distance between eye lens and retina decreases that means the diameter of the eyeball is reduced. The person suffering from hypermetropia can't see the near by objects but can see the distant objects clearly.

question: In which of the following mirror size of image formed is always equal to the size of object?
explanation: A plane mirror due its flat reflective surface forms the image which is equal to the size of the object. The angle of incidence always equal the angle of reflection in such mirrors. The images which are formed by the plane mirror are virtual, upright and left right reservesed.

question: The splitting of white light into its component colours is called…………
explanation: The splitting of white light into its component colours is called Dispersion of light. It is mainly occurs when light ray incident upon a glass prism. • Bending of light, travelling in a straight line from the corner of an obstacle while passing through it is called Diffraction. • When a light ray passes from one transparent medium to another transparent medium perpendicularly at only plain on a line dividing both the mediums, then it deviates from its direction, this is called Refraction of light. • When light waves falls on extremely small bodies such as dust particles etc. Then these are thrown out in all directions this is known as Scattering of light.

question: Which among the following is used to transmit data in optical fibre?
explanation: Light is used to transmit data in optical fibre. An optical fibre is a flexible transparent fiber made by drawing glass (silica) or plastic to a diameter slightly thicker than that of a human hair. Optical fibers are used most often as a means to transmit light between the two ends of the fiber and find wide usage in fiber-optic communications.

question: Which of the following lens always forms a virtual, erect and diminished image?
explanation: A concave lens is a lens that processes at least one surface that curves inwards. A concave lens is thinner at its centre than at its edges and produces on erect and diminishing image. • Concave Lens – A concave lens forms always a virtual, erect and diminished image between the F and 2F of the Lens.

question: Light waves are .........
explanation: There are waves of energy and light moving all around us in the form of TV and audio transmissions, gamma radiation from space, and heat in the atmosphere. In this formulation of electromagnetism, Maxwell described light as a propagating wave of electric and magnetic fields. More generally he predicted the existence of electro-magnetic radiation travelling as waves at a speed equal to known speed of light.

question: Which of the following occurs due to refraction? (I) The apparent displacement of a pencil, partly immersed in water (II) Sun appears before the sunrise (III) Sky appears blue
explanation: Refraction is the bending of wave when it enters a medium where its speed is changed. The refraction of light when it passes from a fast medium to a slow medium bends the light ray towards the normal to the boundary between the two medium. Examples of light refraction in daily life are following: (i) See a filled glass of water. The bottom seems raised. (ii) Put a spoon in a transparent glass of water it looks bent. (iii) Rainbow. • Sky appears blue due to dispersion of light.

question: Which type of image is always formed by a convex mirror? I. Real II. Virtual III. Enlarged
explanation: Convex mirror always formed virtual and erect image. When the object is at the infinity highly diminished, virtual and erect image is formed and when the object is between infinity and pole of a convex mirror, a diminished, virtual and erect image is formed between pole and focus behind the mirror. Convex mirror is used in rear view mirror of vehicles. The field of view is widest in case of convex mirror, which enables it to show a wider area from behind. 500 / 896

question: The eye lens forms which type of image on retina?
explanation: The eye lens forms a Real and Inverted image of the object on retina. The optic nerve carries it in the forms of signals to the brain and our brain interprets this correctly. The ability of the eye to form an image on the curved surface of the retina is extremely important in human vision.

question: Which of the following statement (S) is/are CORRECT for critical angle? I. Refractive angle is 900 II. Light ray goes from rarer to denser medium. III. Light ray goes from denser to rarer medium.
explanation: Critical angle is the angle of incidence beyond which rays of light passing through a denser medium to the surface of less denser medium are no longer refracted but totally reflected. Example:- when the angle of incidence in water reaches a certain critical value, the refracted ray lies along the boundary, having an angle of refraction of 900.

question: A person with hypermetropia………..

options.c: cannot see both nearby and distant objects clearly

explanation: The medical name for long-sight is hypermetropia, some-times called hyperopia. Long sight leads to problems with near vision and the eyes may commonly become tired. Farsightedness can be corrected with convex glasses or contact lenses. • Myopia– A condition in which nearby objects can be seen clearly while the objects at large distance are blurry. Concave glasses or contact lenses are used to correct such problem.

question: In a concave mirror if object is placed at centre of curvature, then image will be………
explanation: Convex and Concave mirrors are a part of spherical mirror where one side is coated with lead oxide. The centre of the glass sphere is called the centre of curvature. In a concave mirror if the object is placed at the centre of curvature, then image will be formed on centre of curvature with equal length and will be real and inverted.

question: In which mirror, image formed is virtual, erect and point sized?
explanation: A convex mirror or diverging mirror or fish eye mirror is a curved mirror in which the reflective surface bulges towards the light source. A parallel beam of light diverges after reflection from a convex mirror. The image formed in convex mirror is behind the mirror between the pole and the focus and the image is formed smaller than the object and it is erect and virtual. These mirrors are utilized in motor cars, trucks etc. as a rear view mirror.

question: Apparent brightness of diamond is due to
explanation: Any transparent material has a refractive index and have critical angle where light totally reflects internally on the surface. Because of this diamonds are cut accordingly with exact angles. After cutting diamond with the techniques it reflects light so that it appears to be shining.

question: Artificial light can
explanation: Sunlight is best in plants but it is also used successfully for photosynthesis than artificial light.

question: By increasing the intensity of incident light on the surface, the photo electric current-
explanation: Photoelectric current is the current through a photosensitive device such as a photodiode which produces electric signals on the application of radiant light energy. • By increasing the intensity of incident light on the surface, the photoelectric current first increase due to increase in accelerating potential. The current can reach a maximum value called saturation current. • Beyond the value, the photoelectric current starts decreasing because of the saturation and falls to zero value.

question: The phenomenon of light splitting into seven distincolours when it passes through prism is –
explanation: Dispersion is the phenomenon of splitting up of composite light into its constituent colours. During dispersions white light is split into its 7 constituent colours.

question: The sun and the moon appear elliptical near horizontal due to :
explanation: When the sun or moon is near the horizon, the rays from the lower edge are bent more than the rays from the upper edge due to atmospheric refraction. This shortens the vertical diameter, causing the sun or moon to appear elliptical. Note : Before sunset, the elliptical nature of sun is due to refraction phenomenon as the ray of light bends before reaching the observer.

question: Endoscopy, a technique used to explore the stomach or other inner Parts of the body, is based on the phenomenon of-
explanation: The Endoscopy produces uses an endoscope to examine the interior of the hollow organ or cavity of the body by the phenomenon of total internal reflection.

question: The coloured lights, most effective in photosynthesis, are
explanation: Red light is more effective in photo- synthesis because both the photosystems (PS-I and PS- II) absorb light of wavelengths in the red region (680 & 700nm, respectively). Blue light is absorbed by carotenoids, which pass the energy to the chlorophyll. Science

question: When an object is placed between two parallel plane mirrors, then the number of images formed is
explanation: When an object is placed between two parallel plane mirrors, the number of images formed is infinite. The image formed by a plane mirror is virtual. The image made by a plane mirror is the same size as that of the object.

question: Red light is used as danger signal because

options.d: It can undergo total internal reflection.

explanation: Red light has the longest wavelength and hence is scattered least by fog or smoke. Hence, Red light can be clearly seen even from a distance. So danger signal lights are usually red in colour.

question: Consider following statements : Assertion (A) : The upper surface of the wings of an aeroplane is made convex and the lower surface is made concave. Reason (R) : The air current at the top has less velocity and thus less pressure at the bottom than at the top. Choose your answer with the help of given code:

options.a: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
options.b: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)

explanation: The Bernoulli theorem is used to make the wings of aircraft. The shape of the wings is kept in such a way that the curvature of the upper surface is more than the curvature of the lower surface and the front edge is round and flat. Since the flow of air above and below the wing is linear. For this reason, the air hitting the upper wing has to travel more than the bottom. As a result, the velocity of the air passing over the wing will be greater than the velocity of the air passing through the bottom. 502 / 896

question: LED is a

options.b: Diode that conducts electricity when light falls on it
options.c: Diode that emits light when a current is passed through it
options.d: Special device that shines when kept in dark

explanation: A Light Emitting Diode (LED) is a semiconductor device, which can emit light when an electric current passes through it. To do this holes from p-Type semiconductor & recombine with elections from n-Type semiconductors to produce light.

question: Laser is a device for producing
explanation: A laser is a device for producing stimulated emissions of electromagnetic radiation. LASER stands for light amplification by stimulated emission of (electromagnetic) radiation.

question: Which of the following can help in reducing the carbon footprint? 1. Using fly-ash based cement in building construction 2. Using LED-based electric lamps instead of incandescent lamps Select the correct answer using the code given below :
explanation: Carbon footprints is the total amount of Greenhouse Gases (carbon dioxide and methane) released into the atmosphere by different human activities. Using fly-ash based cement in building construction and using LED-based electric lamps instead of incandescent lamps can help in reducing carbon footprint.

question: Which of the following colours of light travels slowest in glass?

options.d: All travels with the same speed

explanation: Violet colour of light travels slowest in glass because speed of light depends upon the wavelength of light. Violet light have the least wavelength. Thus, it travels slowest in glass. Red colour of light travels fastest in glass.

question: Optical fiber works on the principle of :
explanation: Optical fiber is a device which works on the Principle of total internal reflection by which light signals can be transmitted from one place to another with a negligible loss of energy. Science

question: Night vision Cameras use :
explanation: Night vision Cameras use infrared light to illuminate images in the dark. Infrared radiation cameras detect these invisible infrared wavelengths enabling camera to see in the dark.

question: Mirage is an example of :
explanation: Mirage is an example of total internal reflection. Which occurs due to atmospheric refraction. Mirage–A mirage is a naturally occurring optical phenomenon in which light rays bend via refraction to produce a displaced image of distant objects or the sky.

question: Generally, what is the colour of emerald?
explanation: The most desirable emerald colours are bluish green to pure green with vivid colour saturation and tone that's not too dark. The most prized emeralds are highly transparent.

question: The colour of a star is an indication of its
explanation: The colour of a star mostly indicates a star's temperature and it can also suggest the star's age. Stars having blue colour are hot and the stars having red colour are cold, stars which are generally dwarf stars. Scientists have categorized the stars into class O, B and M, where class O and B stars are the hottest (blue in colour) and class M starts are the coolest ones (red in colour).

question: Assertion (A): Grease is used in heavy machinery as lubricant. Reason (R): Grease is highly viscous and reduces friction.

options.a: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
options.b: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)

explanation: Grease is used in heavy machinery as lubricant because grease is highly viscous and reduces friction. Grease is a solid or semisolid lubricant formed as a dispersion of thickening agents in a liquid lubricant. Grease generally consists of a soap emulsified with mineral or vegetable oil. A common feature of greases is that they possess a high initial viscosity, which upon the application of shear, drops to give the effect of an oil lubricated bearing of approximately the same viscosity as the base oil used in the grease. 503 / 896

question: Consider the following statements and select your answer : Assertion (A): Fraunhofer lines are dark lines in solar spectrum. Reason (R): They are absorption lines due to the elements present in earth's atmosphere.

options.a: Both (A) and (R) are individually true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
options.b: Both (A) and (R) are individually true and (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)

explanation: Fraunhofer lines, in astronomical spectroscopy, any of the dark (absorption) lines in the spectrum of the Sun or other Star, caused by selective absorption of the Sun's or Star's radiation at specific wavelengths by the various elements existing as gases in its atmosphere.

question: Rainbow is formed due to _____

options.a: absorption of sunlight in minute was droplets
options.b: diffusion of sunlight through water droplets
options.d: refraction and reflection of sunlight by water droplets

explanation: Rainbow is a spectrum of light in the form of circular arc caused due to reflection, refraction and dispersion of light. It represents visibility of all 7 colours of bright white light. Rainbows caused by sunlight always appears in the section of sky directly opposite the sun.

question: Stars twinkle because _____

options.a: the intensity of light emitted by them changes with time
options.b: the distance of the stars from the earth changes with time
options.c: the refractive index of the different layers of the earth's atmosphere changes continuously, consequently the position of the image of a star changes with time
options.d: the light from the star is scattered by the dust particles and air molecules in the earth's atmosphere

explanation: The physical conditions of the atmosphere keep on changing continuously due to which density of air in different layers of atmosphere also keep on changing. As a result of this, light coming from stars suffer multiple refraction. Due to this fluctuation perceived brightness of the star, appear like they are twinkling.

question: Which of the following light phenomena take place in a periscope? a. Reflection b. Dispersion c. Refraction d. Diffraction Select the code for the correct answer from the options given below:

options.d: a, b, c and d Science

explanation: A Periscope is an optical instrument that uses a system of prism, lenses or mirrors to reflect images through a tube. A periscope is useful example of the law of reflection at work. It is important that your mirrors are placed at a 450 angle because light always reflects away from a mirror at the same angle that it hits it. In a periscope, light from an object strikes the top 450 mirror at and bounces off at the same angle.

question: In an optical fibre, the refractive index of the core

options.a: is greater than that of the cladding
options.b: is less than that of the cladding
options.c: and the cladding is the same

explanation: An optical fibres the refractive index of core is higher than that of the cladding. So, light in the core that strikes the boundary with the cladding at an angle shallower than critical angle will be reflected back into the core by total internal reflection. In optical fiber, the refractive index of the inner part is 1.68 and the refractive index of water parts is 1.44.

question: Compact fluorescent lamps pose problems of disposal, because of
explanation: Compact fluorescent light bulbs (CFLs) use dramatically less energy than incandescent bulbs and reduce greenhouse gas emissions. But they also contain mercury a dangerous toxin. So, there is problem of disposal.

question: At 0 K, silicon behaves as a/an
explanation: Insulator– Silicon behaves as an insulator at zero Kelvin because the electrons do not possess sufficient thermal energy to overcome the forbidden energy gap. Conductor– A conductor is an object or type of material that allows the flow of charge in one or more directions. Semiconductor– The forbidden energy gap is inconsequential. The valence band almost completely overlap with the conduction band at room temperature. Superconductor– In a superconductor, an electric current can exist forever. It is a material that achieves superconductivity, which is a state of matter that has no electrical resistance and does not allow magnetic fields to penetrate. 504 / 896

question: Which of the following converts AC voltage into DC ?
explanation: Rectifier converts AC voltage into DC . Rectifier :– It is a device that converts an oscillating two-directional alternating current into single - directional direct current. Amplifier :– An amplifier is an electronic device that can increase the power of a signal. Transformer– A transformer is a device that transfers electrical energy one AC circuit to one or more other circuits either increasing (step-up) or reducing (step down) the voltage. Oscillator:- It basically converts unidirectional current flow from a DC source into an alternating waveform.

question: LED and LCD stand for?

options.a: Light Emitting Display and Liquid Crystal Display
options.b: Light Emitting Diode and Liquid Crystal Diode
options.c: Light Emitting Diode and Liquid Crystal Display
options.d: Light Emitting Detector and Liquid Crystal Detector

explanation: LED stand for Light Emitting Diode while LCD is short form of Liquid Crystal Display. The difference between two is the placement and type of light used. LEDs are also slimmer than LCDs and provide a better quality, clearer picture with high definition output. LCD stand for Liquid Crystal Display is a flat-panel display or other electronically modulated optical device that uses the light-modulating properties of Liquid crystal combined with polarizers.

question: The non-metal that conducts electricity is
explanation: Graphite is a non-metal and it is the only non-metal that can conduct electricity. Graphite is a form of carbon which is an element. In graphite, the carbon atoms are joined together and arranged in layers. The links between the carbon atoms in the layer are strong, but the links between the layers are weak.

question: Under the influence of a transverse magnetic field, an electron moves in a circle with the constant speed 'v'. The time period of revolution is
explanation: In a Uniform magnetic field, a charged particle is moving in a circle of radius R with constant speed v. mv2 ∵ =Bqv R Science mv R= …..(i) Bq 2πR 2πmv 2πm Time period, T= = = .....(ii) v Bqv Bq Time Period T does not depend on both R and v because v is changed and R is also changed.

question: The stopping potential value of a material is 0.6 V when the light source is kept at a distance 'X' cm. If the source is moved to '2X' cm away, the stopping potential will be
explanation: Stopping potential does not depend on the relative distance between the source and the cell. For given intensity for radiation the stopping potential depend upon the frequency. Higher the frequency of incident light higher the value of stopping potential. So if the source is moved to '2X' cm away, the stopping potential will be 0.6V.

question: Which of the following is NOT an electrical insulator?
explanation: Gold is not an electric insulator. The Substance which have extremely high resistance and through which current cannot flow are called an insulator. Diamond, rubber and Glass are insulators. Gold is a good conductor of electricity. An electrical conductor is a substance in which electrical charge carriers, usually electrons, move easily from atom to atom with the application of voltage. Good conductors of electricity ; Gold, Silver, Copper, Aluminum and Graphite.

question: Semiconductor diodes can be used in the following circuits
explanation: A diode is a semiconductor device that essentially act as one way switch current. It allows current to flow easily in one direction but severely restricts current from flowing in the opposite direction. Semiconductor diodes can be used in the following circuits. Rectifiers, clipper circuits, clamping circuits, logic gates, LED lamps, voltage Multipliers etc.

question: The SI unit of electric charge is :
explanation: The SI unit of electric charge is Coulomb. A coulomb is defined as the charge transferred by a current of 1 ampere flowing in one second. A Coulomb is a quantity measurement for electrons. One Coulomb 1018 contains 6.24 × electrons. Charge on 1 proton is 1.6 × 10–19 C.

question: Who discovered the photoelectric effect?
explanation: The discovery of the Photoelectric effect is ascribed to the German Physicist Heinrich Hertz (1857- 1894) in 1887, he observed that Ultraviolet radiation lowers the voltage at which sparking takes places between two electrodes. The Photoelectric effect is defined as the emission of electric charges (electrons, Ions) resulting from the absorption of Electromagnetic radiation (Infrared, Visible, Ultraviolet, X-Ray) in a material (solid, liquid or gas). Note : Albert Einstein explained the photoelectric effect by introducing early quantum ideas in 1905.

question: In the context of the units for measuring electric power, which of the following statements is NOT correct?

options.a: The unit of electric energy is watt hour (Wh).
options.c: The SI unit for measuring electric power is Watt.
options.d: A Watt is the unit of electrical power equal to one ampere under the pressure of one volt.

explanation: • Electricity is measured in Watts and Kilowatts. • Watt (W) is the unit of Electricity. It measures the rate of consumed energy. [1W = 1J/1sec] [1W = 1V.1A] • 1 Kilowatt = 1000 Watts (W)

question: R–C coupling is used for
explanation: R–C coupling is the most widely used as voltage amplifiers. In this case the resistance R is the resistor connected at the collector terminal and the capacitor C is connected in between the amplifiers.

question: To save electricity LEDs are preferred over conventional bulbs. The full form of LED is
explanation: LED is known as "Light Emitting Diode". In devices, it is biggest invention so far, which gives the highest energy and light. These energy devices are becoming very popular in different areas, such as traffic signals, mining industries, elevators etc. It does not have mercury like CFL bulbs, but it contains other harmful components such as lead and nickel.

question: The element with highest electrical conductivity is
explanation: Silver has the highest electrical conductivity of all metals. In fact silver defines conductivity-all other metals are compared against it, on a scale of 0 to 100 silver ranks 100 while Copper at 97 and Gold at 76. Science

question: Which object of everyday use has types AA, AAA, C and D?
explanation: AA, AAA, C and D type batteries are used in our everyday life. We are probably familiar with AAA, AA, C and D batteries. These letters are indicator of size. These are single use batteries. Diagram SIM means Subscriber Identity Module and it is a small plastic card that slots into your phone. SIMs are like computer chips which hold information and allow you to connect with your network. Memory card– A memory card is electronic data storage device used for storing digital information using flash memory. Batteries– A battery is a device consisting of one or more electrochemical cells with external connections for powering electrical devices such as flashlights, mobile phones and electric cars etc. Bond paper– Bond paper is a high-quality durable writing paper similar to bank paper but having weight greater than 50 g/m2. The name comes from its having originally been made for documents such as government bonds.

question: When the direction of motion of coil is perpendicular to the electromagnetic field, then the value of induced current is………..
explanation: The direction of motion of coil is perpendicular to the electromagnetic field, then the value of induced current is maximum. This law was given by Michael Faraday according to which "A changing magnetic field through a coil of wire therefore must induce an emf in the coil which in turn causes current to flow." It is also called Henry’s Law.

question: When two resistors are connected in parallel, they have ........

options.d: Both same current and different potential difference 506 / 896

explanation: When the two resistors are in series : (1) Current (i) is same in both. (2) Voltage (v) is different in both. When two resistors are in parallel: (1) Current (i) is different in both. (2) Voltage (v) is same in both. Thus, resistors connected in parallel have equal potential differences across them.

question: What precaution should be taken to avoid the overloading of domestic electric circuits?

options.a: Fuse should be connected in the circuit
options.b: Too many appliances should not be connected in single circuit
options.c: Faulty appliances should not be connected in the circuit

explanation: To avoid the overloading of domestic electric circuits: (1) Too many appliances should not be connected in single socket. (2) Faulty appliances should not be connected to the circuit. (3) T h e F u s e s h o u l d b e c o n n e c te d in the circuit so that an y s h o r t c i r cu i t i n g i s a v o id e d .

question: What will be equivalent resistance of two resistors R and R when connected in series? 1 2
explanation: Equivalent resistance when: (i) Resistor R & R connected in series, R = R + R 1 2 1 2 1 1 (ii) Resistor R & R connected in parallel, R= + 1 2 R R 1 2

question: The phenomena of electromagnetic induction is ..........

options.a: The process of charging a body
options.b: The process of generating magnetic field due to a current passing through a coil
options.c: Induced current in a coil due to relative motion between a magnet and the coil
options.d: The process of rotating a coil of an electric motor

explanation: Whenever there is a relative motion between a magnet and a coil, a current is induced in the coil, this phenomenon is known as Electromagnetic induction in which the electric current is induced in the coil due to magnetic flux linkage. Science

question: There is a circular coil having n turns. The field produced is how many times than produced by a coil of single turn?
explanation: When a current is passed through the circular loop, a magnetic field is produced around it. The magnetic field lines would be in the form of concentric circles around carrying circular loop, the concentric circles representing the magnetic field around it would become larger & larger as we move away from the wire. The magnetic field at point is addition of the field produced by each turn. Therefore is there are 'n' turns of coil, magnitude of magnetic field will be n times of magnetic field in case of single turn of coil.

question: Which among the following is NOT a conductor of electricity?
explanation: Ebonite is made up of special vulcanized rubber. Hence, ebonite is not a metal and does not have free electrons to carry current that's why ebonite is not a good conductor of electricity or we can say ebonite is an insulator.

question: Which of the following statement is INCORECT?

options.a: An electric motor converts electric energy into mechanical energy.
options.b: An electric generator works on the principle of electromagnetic induction.

question: A change in frequency by scattering (Raman Effect) occurs due to ---------- of energy between the incident photon and the scattering molecule.
explanation: Raman scattering is the inelastic scattering of a photon. However, a small fraction of the scattered photons are inelastically scattered with a frequency different from and usual less than, that of the incident photons.

question: Which of the following has most penetrating power?

question: What is the repetition of sound, produced by the reflection of sound waves?

question: Which of the following represents the distance travelled by a wave in one second?
explanation: Wave velocity:- The distance travelled by a wave in one second is called the velocity of that wave. Its S.I. unit is meter per second (m/s). Wavelength:- The distance travelled by the wave in one time period of vibration of particle of medium is called its wavelength. Its S.I. unit is meter (m). Frequency:- The number of vibrations made by a particle of the medium in one second is called the frequency of the waves. Its S.I. unit is hertz (Hz) or Per sec. Time Period:- A time period is the time taken for one complete cycle of vibration to pass a given point. It is denoted by T. Its S.I. unit is sec.

question: Which of the following determines the loudness or softness of a sound?
explanation: Loudness is physiological response of the ear to the intensity of sound. It distinguishes between a loud sound and a low sound. Loudness depends on two factors : (i) Intensity of sound which is directly proportional to the square of amplitude of the sound wave. (ii) Sensitivity of the ear.

question: What is the repeated reflection of sound from the walls of a large hall, which results in the persistance of sound?
explanation: Reverberation : When a sound is produced in a big hall or auditorium, the sound waves suffer multiple reflection from the walls, ceilings and other materials present in the hall. Due to this the sound persists for some time even after the source of sound has stopped producing sound. This persistence of sound due to repeated reflection is called reverberation.

question: Which technique is used to determine the depth of the ocean, explore underwater hills, valleys, submarines, icebergs etc.?

question: In Physics, the Nobel Prize, 2014 was awarded for the discovery of
explanation: The Noble Prize in Physics 2014 was awarded jointly to Isamu Akasaki, Hiroshi Amano and Shuji Nakamura "For the invention of efficient blue light-emitting diodes which has enabled bright and energy-saving white light sources." The researcher's work has already made it into many • Common Electronic Devices. Blue-LEDs can be found in most touch screen devices. White LEDs usually use a blue LED to excite a phosphor to emit white light, and can be found in the camera flashes of most modern smart phones.

question: Light-emitting diode is an example of
explanation: Light-emitting diode (LED) is an Optoelectronic Semiconductor device which generates light via electroluminescence. It contains a p-n junction, through which an electric current is sent. The current generates electrons and holes, which release their energy portions as photons when they recombine. Example of Optoelectronic Devices are : (i) Light emitting diodes (LED) (ii) Laser diodes (LD) (iii) Super luminescent diodes (SLD).

question: Pneumatic structures are: 1. Membrane structures. 2. Stabilized by compressed air. 3. Characterized by internal pressure being less than external pressure. 4. explanation: Pneumatic structural systems (or Air supported structures) are structures or buildings that utilize air pressure to ensure it's structural integrity. This bubble shape is created by evenly increasing pressure of air inside structure to slightly above atmospheric pressure. Then cables are used to make the exterior membrane more rigid and sturdy to carry the load and then anchored to ground.

question: Which thermodynamic law defines entropy-
explanation: The second law of thermodynamics establishes the concept of entropy as a physical property of a thermodynamic system. Entropy, the measure of a system's thermal energy per unit temperature that is unavailable for doing useful work. Because work is obtained from ordered molecular motion, the amount of entropy is also a measure of the molecular disorder, or randomness, of a system.

question: When solar radiation impinges on a snow surface, then :

explanation: If a solar radiation impinges on a snow surface, then most of the radiation is reflected back and a little portion is absorbed because snow, ice and clouds have high albedos (from 0.7 to 0.9) and reflect more energy than they absorb. Earth's average albedo is about 0.3. In other words, about 30% of incoming solar radiation is reflected back into space and 70% absorbed.

question: What is the average temperature of the sun's surface in our solar system?

question: Mohs' scale is a scale for testing

explanation: Mohs' scale is a scale for testing hardness of material. It is one of the most important tests for identifying mineral specimens. This test compares the resistance of a mineral being scratched by ten reference minerals known as the Mohs Hardness scale. Friedrich Mohs, a German mineralogist, developed the scale in the year 1812.

question: Lightning strikes sometimes explode trees, because

options.a: The lightning bolt strikes very fast
options.b: The leaves become electrically discharged during the lightning strike
options.c: The water inside the tree evaporates too rapidly

explanation: Lightning strikes sometimes explode trees because the water inside the tree evaporates too rapidly. Trees can explode when struck by lightning. The strong electric current is carried mostly by the water-conducting sapwood below the bark, heating it up and boiling the water. The pressure of the steam can make the trunk burst. This happens especially with trees whose trunks are already dying or rotting.

question: Generally, the nucleus of an atom consists of …….
explanation: An atom is the smallest particle of an element that can participate in any chemical reaction. But one cannot remain free. The nucleus of an atom consists of protons and neutrons. A proton is a micro particle inside a proton-atom, whose relative mass is approximately equal to the mass of the hydrogen atom and has a unit positive charge. Goldstein owes the discovery of this particle with positive charge to the atom. Neutron is a micro particle inside a atom whose mass is approximately equal to the mass of the proton. But there is no charge on it. Namely, a neutron is a neutral particle. Neutron was discovered by Chadwick in 1932 by striking α -particles on beryllium metal.94.

question: Which method is used to protect iron from rusting?
explanation: Galvanization is the process of applying a protective zinc coating to steel or iron to prevent rusting. The protection has two folds: (i) Physical protection from moisture and air. (ii) Electrochemical cathodic protection. The most generally adopted galvanization is hot-dip galvanization. Galvanization – Galvanizing coats iron or steel in zinc • to protect from rust.

question: Which of the following is an example of colloidal solution of liquid in solid?
explanation: A colloidal solution, sometimes known as a colloidal suspension, is a solution in which a material is evenly suspended in the liquid. In other words, a colloid is a microscopically small substance that is equally dispersed throughout another material. When dispersion medium is solid, the dispersed phase can be solid, liquid or gas. (i) Solid – Gemstones, Pearls etc. (ii) Liquid – Cheese, Butter etc... (iii) Gas – Pumic stone, Foam rubber etc.

question: In a reaction, what is the loss of hydrogen by substance called as?
explanation: The original view of oxidation & reduction is that of adding or removing oxygen. question: When a substance loses oxygen during a reaction, it is called____.
explanation: Reduction is the type of chemical reaction in which loss of oxygen atom from a molecule or the gaining of one or more electrons. A reduction reaction is seen from the point of view of the molecule being reduced, as when one molecule gets reduced another gets oxidized. The full reaction is known as a redox reaction. • Corrosion is a natural process that converts a refined metal into a more chemically stable form such as oxide, hydroxide, carbonate or sulfide. • Oxidation is the loss of electrons during a reaction by a molecule, atom or ion. Note : An oxidation-reduction (redox) reaction is a type of chemical reaction that involves a transfer of electrons between two species.

question: In which type of chemical reaction a single product is formed from two or more reactants?
explanation: In a combination reaction (also known as synthesis reaction) two or more simple substances combine to form a more complex substance. Two or more reactants yielding one product is another way to identify a synthesis reaction. Such reaction may be represented by equations of the following form : x+y → xy. • Decomposition reactions are a type of chemical reaction that involves breaking down a substance into smaller compounds or individual elements.

question: Iron rusts because of……….reaction.
explanation: Rust is an iron oxidation process where red oxide is formed by the redox reaction of iron and oxygen in the presence of water or air moisture. There are several methods to prevent iron rusting– Galvanization, Cathodic Protection, Coating and Painting, Bluing, Inhibitors etc.

question: The souring of milk to curd is an example of –

It is • the hydrolysis of an ester with NaOH or KOH to give alcohol and sodium or potassium salt of the acid. • Putrefaction is the process of decay or rotting in a body or other organic matter. • Esterification is the chemical process that combines alcohol (ROH) and an organic acid (RCOOH) to from an ester (RCOOR) and water.

question: Which among the following is not a road junction at the same elevation?

question: Of the following types of chemical compounds, which one is a key ingredient in the formation of photochemical smog?

It is a reverse of evaporation. • Deposition is the geological process in which sediments, soil and rocks are added to a landform or landmass.

question: In which one of the following types of bonds, the bond formation is by free moving electrons in an array of positive ions ?
explanation: Metallic Bonds– In metals, each atom looses all its valence electrons and hence, becomes a positively charged ion. These valence electrons from an electron gas or cloud throughout the space occupied by the atoms. The valence electrons move freely within the crystal due to the influence of other atoms and are not bonded to individual atoms. • Metallic bonds are formed while sharing a variable number of electrons by a variable number of atoms. It is weaker than ionic bond and covalent bonds, but stronger than van der waals type bonds. • Covalent or Homopolar Bonds– A covalent bond is formed when two or more similar atoms form molecules by sharing their valence electrons. Therefore, it is called as homopolar or homonuclear bonding.

question: Additional of chlorine to raw water before treatment is known as –

question: Elements with valency one are :
explanation: Elements with valency 1 are those elements which can either gain one electron or lose one electron in order to have stable Electronic Configuration. Example is chlorine with atomic number 17. It has electronic configuration as 2,8,7. Hence, it accepts one Cl– electron to form and attains stable octet configuration. Such elements are non metals. Whereas, elements like sodium with atomic number 11 has electronic configuration 2,8,1. It is easier to lose the Na+. outermost electron to become stable and form Such elements are metals. But Tetravalent element (mean element have valency 4) neither gain nor lose their electron. They share their electron to get stable or complete their octet. Example carbon.

question: What is the key ingredient in vinegar that you use in your diet?
explanation: Vinegar is an acid with its key ingredient, acetic acid. It is widely used for food preservation and cooking. Acetic acid content are available for agriculture or cleaning purposes. It is most commonly made by the fermentation of grain alcohol.

question: Lactic acid can be produced from :
explanation: Rhizopus oryzae is capable of producing high levels of lactic acid by the fermentation of glucose. Streptococcus lactis is an important bacteria responsible for manufacturing of dairy products. It breaks the sugar present in the milk and produces lactic acid.

question: Which of the following air pollutants are responsible for acid rain within and downwind areas of major industrial emission?

options.a: H S and oxides of Nitrogen 2

explanation: Acid rain is caused by a chemical reaction that begins when compounds like sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxides are released into the air. These substances can rise very high into the atmosphere, where they mix and react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form more acidic pollutants, known as acid rain.

question: Acids found in the acid rain water are :
explanation: Acid rain results when sulphur dioxide (SO ) 2 and Nitrogen dioxide (NO) are emitted into the 2 atmosphere and transported by wind and air currents. The SO and NO react with water, oxygen and other chemicals 2 2 to form sulphuric and nitric acids. These mix with water and other materials before falling to the ground. While a small portion of the SO and NO that cause acid rain is 2 2 from natural sources such as volcanoes, most of it comes from the burning of fossil fuels.

question: Which of the following are examples of basic amino acids?
explanation: There are Arginine, Lysine, Histidine of example of basic amino acids. • Amino acid are type of molecules which combine form protein. • When protein broken down and digest in human body it convert into amino acid as used as building block of body. • The 9 essential Amino Acid are : Histidine, Isoleucine, Leucine, Lysine, Methionine, Phenylalanine, Threonine, Tryptophan and Valine. 589 / 896

question: Noble Gases 995. Which one of the following gases was discovered first in the chromosphere of the Sun?
explanation: Helium was discovered in 1868 from emission lines observed in the chromosphere during an eclipse. Helium is a chemical element, which usually remains in gaseous state. It is an inert gas or Nobel gas and is also colourless, odourless, tasteless and toxic. Its atomic number is 2. Its boiling point and melting point is the lowest among all elements.

question: The inert gas produced in the nuclear fusion process is
explanation: A fusion reactor produces helium, which is an inert gas. It also produces and consumes tritium within the plant. In a closed circuit, helium gas is used for welding metals such as aluminum, in rocket propulsion, in metrology etc.

question: Stranger gas is
explanation: Xenon is called a stranger gas because the element name has been derived from the Greek Word "Xenos" which translates to 'stranger'. Apart from it, It belongs to the noble gas group where elements are very non reactive.

explanation: Brass, alloy of Copper and Zinc, of historical and enduring importance because of its hardness and workability. The earliest brass, called calamine brass, dates to Neolithic times; it was probably made by reduction of mixtures of Zinc ores and Copper ores.

Hence, brass gets discoloured when it is exposed to air. (cid:1) Brass is an alloy of copper and zinc.

question: In treatment of water what for sedimentation with coagulation is done? 592 / 896
explanation: The coagulation process involves adding iron or aluminium salts, such as aluminium sulphate, ferric sulphate, ferric chloride or polymers, to the water. question: Which of the following is pure and free from impurities?
explanation: Distilled water has been boiled into vapour and condensed back into liquid in a separate container. • Impurities in the original water don't blow below or near the boiling point of water remain in the original container. Thus distilled water is one type of purified water.

question: Bhopal gas tragedy occurred due to leakage of
explanation: Post midnight on December 3, 1984,Methyl isocyanate (MIC) (Chemical formula– (CH NCO or 3 C H NO) leaked from the pesticide plant of Union 2 3 Carbide India limited, (UCIL) in M.P. (Madhya Pradesh's capital Bhopal) Methyl isocyanate is extremely toxic gas and if its concentration in the air touches 21 PPM (parts per million), it can cause death within minutes of inhaling the gas. Bhopal Gas tragedy was India's first major industrial disaster. At least 30 tonnes of methyl isocyanate gas killed more than 15,000 people and affected over 600,000 workers. Bhopal gas tragedy is known as world's worst industrial disaster.

question: Which of the following methods can be used to increase the Octane rating of gasoline?

question: Miscellaneous 1043. The elements like silicon, tellurium and germanium can be purified by

options.a: Electrolytic Refining–Refining the metal by the
options.c: Pattinsons Process–A method for removing silver
options.d: Pattinson's process process of electrolysis. from lead.

explanation: Zone-refining is used to obtain metal of very high purity. The principle on which it is based on that, an impure molten metal on gradual cooling will deposit its crystals of pure metal, while the impurities will be left in the remaining part of molten metal. Silicon, Tellurium and Germanium can be purified by this method.

question: What is the major commercial source of thorium?
explanation: Thorium is more abundant in nature than uranium. It is fertile rather than fissile, and can only be used as a fuel in conjunction with a fissile material such as recycled plutonium. The most common source of thorium is the rare earth phosphate mineral, monazite, which contains up to about 12% thorium phosphate, but 6-7% on average.

question: Who has formulated the periodic table for chemical element?

question: The pH value of fertile soil is usually around
explanation: The pH value of fertile soil is usually around 6.5 to 7.5 pH range. It has determined that most plant nutrients are optimally available to the plants. Plants grow best in neutral or slightly acidic pH. Slight acidity is favors tree growth and forms forests. Slight alkalinity is however, helpful in growth of grasslands and some crops plants like legumes. Hence, for most plants a pH value of 6 to 7 is favorable. Note- pH scale, logarithmic and inversely indicate the concentration of hydrogen ions in the solution. The range goes from 0 to 14, with 7 being neutral, pH of less than 7 indicates acidity whereas pH of greater than 7 indicates a base.

question: With respect to humans, why is carbon monoxide a pollutant?
explanation: Carbon monoxide is a colorless, odorless and tasteless gas that is slightly less dense than air. It is toxic to Remoglobic animals, including humans. It is also called flue gas. Through respiration it reaches in the body and combines with haemoglobin to produce carboxy haemoglobin, which usurps the space in haemoglobin that normally carries oxygen, but is in effective for delivering oxygen which causes death. Carbon monoxide reacts with haemoglobin 10 times higher than oxygen.

question: The Science of Dactylography is commonly known as
explanation: The scientific study of fingerprints, which is known as dactylography, is used as a technique of crime detection by practically every modern law enforcement agency. • Carbon-dating – A method of calculating the age of very old objects.

question: Ice cream belongs to which type of following colloid?
explanation: Ice-cream is an example of an Emulsion. A combination of two liquid that don't normally mix together and are dispersed throughout each other. In ice-cream, molecules of fat are suspended in a water- sugar-ice structure along with air bubbles. Note : Colloid in chemistry, are a mixture of two substances, in which one substance is divided into minute particles. The types of colloids include sol, emulsion, foam and aerosol. Sol is a colloidal suspension with solid particles in a liquid. Emulsion is between two liquids. Foam is formed when many gas particles are trapped in a liquid or solid. Aerosol contains small particles of liquid or solid dispersed in gas.

question: Consider the following statements about catalysts: a. Catalysts are not consumed during chemical reactions. b. Catalysts alter the Rate of the reactions. c. Catalysts are required in stoichiometric amounts. 601 / 896 Which of the above statements is/ are correct? Select the code for the correct answer from the options given below:
explanation: Catalyst is any substance that increases the rate of reaction without itself being consumed. A catalyst increases the rate of a reaction, and it also improves the yield of the intended products, A catalyst actually takes part in the reaction even though it itself is not consumed or used up in the course of the reaction. A stoichiometric amount of one reactant can mean the amount that will react completely with a given amount of another reactant, leaving no excess of either one. Note:– A stoichiometric reactant is a reactant that is consumed in the overall reaction because it reacts in one step and is regenerated in another step.

question: Natural ripening of climacteric fruits (like apple) occurs due to

options.a: Release of ethylene and increased rate of respiration
options.b: Release of ethylene and decreased rate of respiration
options.c: Release of acetylene and increased rate of reparation
options.d: Release of acetylene and decreased rate of respiration

explanation: The first group is called the climacteric fruits, in which ripening are accompanied by a burst of ethylene. These fruits can also respond to external ethylene by increasing their ripening rate. These include fleshy fruits, such as tomato, avocado, apple, melon peach, kiwi, and banana.

question: Graphene can be used as base material for making
explanation: Graphene has a high carrier mobility and low noise allowing it to be used as the channel in a field-effect transistor. Graphene is an allotrope of carbon consisting of a single layer of atoms arranged in a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice. Potential graphene applications include lightweight, thin, and flexible electric/photonics circuits, solar cells, and various medical, chemical and industrial processes enhanced or enabled by the use of new graphene materials.

question: Photovoltaic cells are generally made of
explanation: Photovoltaic cell can be manufactured in a variety of ways and from many different material. The most common material for commercial solar cell construction is Silicon (Si), but others include Gallium Arsenide (GaAs), Cadmium Telluride (CdTe) and Copper Indium Gallium Selenide (CIGS).

question: Which of the following is also known as God particle?
explanation: In 2012, scientists confirmed the detection of the long-sought Higgs boson, also known by its nickname the "God particle," at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), the most powerful particle accelerator on the planet. This particle helps give mass to all elementary particles that have mass, such as electrons and protons.

question: The radioactive isotope used to diagnose thyroid problem is :
explanation: Radioactive isotope is Iodine-131, which has a half life of eight days. It is employed in medicine to monitor thyroid gland functioning, to treat goiter and thyroid cancer, and to locate tumors of the brain and of the liver. It is also used in investigations to trace the course of compounds in Metabolism.

explanation: The system of animal classification used now-a-days is Natural classification. It is based on a large number of characters. question: The aqueous humor is a part of which body organ of human beings?
explanation: The Aqueous humor is part of eye organ of human beings. It is middle of the cornea of human eye and the eye lens is filled with transparent fluid like water. Vitreous Humor : The space behind the lens to the retina is filled with a thick transparent and high reactive index fluid, called vitreous humor.

question: The folding of the leaf lobes of the Venus flag trap is a response to changing
explanation: The folding of the leaf lobes of the Venus flag trap is a response to changing. The Venus flytrap is a flowering plant best known for its carnivorous eating habits. A Venus flytrap leaf has two lobes connected at a hinge on its stalk. The lobes of flytrap open wide and on them are short, stiff- hairs (cilia hairs) called trigger or sensitive hairs. When anything touches (insects) these hairs enough to bend them, the two lobes of the leaves snap shut trapping whatever is inside. The trap will shut in less than a second.

options.d: Fungi do not have cell wall

question: Pork eaters are prone to be infected with :-

question: Bagasse is by-product of :

question: Which of the only bird that can fly backwards?
explanation: Humming birds are the only birds that can fly backwards and upside down. The design of a humming bird's wings differs from most other types of birds. Humming bird's have a unique ball and socket joint at the shoulder that allows the bird to rotate its wings 180 degree in all directions.

question: 'Clove' is a-

question: Which of the following is in monera group?
explanation: Bacteria is placed in Monera group by Whittaker. (cid:1) Monera is a kingdom that contained unicellular organism with a prokaryotic cell organization, such as bacteria. question: In which of the following modes of nutrition do organisms take in nutrients in solution form dead and decaying matter?
explanation: In this type of nutrition, organisms absorb their food from the body of dead animals and plants in the form the surface of their body. Organisms that obtains their food by mortifying nutrition are called saprophytes. Dead bodies are also called Decomposers Science because they break down complex molecules. For example, in fungi, bacteria and same protozoa, nutrition occurs by this method.

question: Which of the following classes of animals are mostly terrestrial, their bodies are covered by dry and cornified skin, epidermal scales or scutes and they do not have external ears?
explanation: Reptiles are mostly terrestrial animal and their body is covered by dry and cornified skin, epidermal scales or scutes. They do not have external ears. Some of the examples of reptiles are Crocodiles, Tortoises, Snakes etc. Mammals are a group of vertebrate animals, characterized by the presence of mammary glands which in females produce milk for feeding their young, a neocortex (brain area), fur or hair and three middle ear bones. Aves, characterized by feathers, toothless beaked jaws, the laying of hard-shelled eggs, a high metabolic rate, a four chambered heart and a strong yet light weight skelton. Birds are a group of warm blooded vertebrates constituting the class Aves. Amphibians are ectothermic, tetrapod vertebrates of the class Amphibia. All living amphibians belong to the group Lissamphibia. Amphibians inhabit a wide variety of habitats, with most species living within terrestrial, fossorial, arboreal or freshwater aquatic ecosystems.

question: An association of two organisms of different species for mutual benefit, and where the individuals may not be able to survive separately, is called
explanation: (cid:1) Symbiosis–Symbiosis refers to relationships between organisms of different species that show an intimate association with each other. For the other species, the relationship may be positive, negative or neutral. (cid:1) Parasitism–Parasitism is a symbiotic relationship in which one species (the parasite) benefits while the other species (the host) is harmed. Some parasites live on the surface of their host. Others live inside their host. They may enter the host through a break in the skin or in food or water. (cid:1) Commensalism– Commensalism is a long term biological interaction in which members of one species gain benefits while those of the other species neither benefit nor are harmed. (cid:1) Not living or occurring in a state a mutualism or symbiosis are called non symbiotic. 605 / 896

question: Which of the following birds produces milk?
explanation: Male and female pigeons produce milk, in addition to this, flamingos and penguins are also birds that produce milk. The best known of these secretions is the 'crop milk'. The milk is produced by a sloughing of fluid-filled cells from the lining of the crop, a thin walled food storage chamber that projects outward from the bottom of the esophagus.

question: Which of the following birds can run at a speed of 40 miles per hour?
explanation: Ostrich can run at a speed of 40 mph. The ostrich with one of its long protruding necks and a densely rounded body is one of the most distinctive birds in the world.

question: Cockroach to :
explanation: Cockroaches are Omnivorous and who can eat almost any type of organic material to survive, making them one of the healthiest insects on the planet. Although they prefer sweets, meats and starches, they also consume other items such as hair, books and decaying Matter. • Sanguivorous :- These animals that solely feed upon the blood of other animals. For example: Vampire bats, white winged vampire bats. • Carnivorous :- A Carnivores is an animal that gets food from killing and eating other animals. Carnivorous generally eat Herbivores, but can eat Omnivores, and occasionally other Carnivores. • Herbivorous :- Herbivore is an animal or insect that only eats vegetation.

question: Which one of the following animals is an Osmoconformer?
explanation: Osmoconformers are marine organism that maintain an internal environment which is isotropic to their external environment. Hagfish is an osmoconformer.

question: In which of the following reptiles is the heart 4- chambered structure?
explanation: The crocodiles are only reptile that has a four- chambered heart (two atria and two ventricles) – • Because of the most complicated blood circulation of all vertebrates. They spent most of their time under water, so this adaptation (four-chambered) help to a reduced rate of circulation and save oxygen. The circulation rate, in water, is reduced by reducing the heartbeat by 2 or 3 minutes. As soon as they come out of the water, the circulation rate comes to normal and requires more oxygen. Hence, the heart has four-chambered to provide a sufficient amount of oxygen. The crocodiles have all other features of reptiles except the heart structure.

question: How many chambers does a cow's stomach have?
explanation: The cow has four stomachs and undergoes a special digestive process to break down the tough and coarse food it eats. It has four compartments made is of Rumen, Reticulum, Omasum and Abomasum.

question: The release of which one of the following into ponds and wells helps in controlling the mosquitoes?
explanation: Gambusia fish is released into ponds and wells to controlling the mosquitoes by eating mosquito larvae insects and crustaceans. Gambusia affinis is an larvivorous fish.

question: In terms of organic evolution, which one of the following is more advanced than the other three?
explanation: Bat's Scientific Name is Chiroptera. Bat are more advance than the Ostrich, Pigeon and Vulture. The organic evolution can be defined as the changes in the organic composition of the population of the organisms that happened by the influence of the environmental changes.

question: Which of the following is not a mammal?
explanation: Great Indian Bustard is not a mammal it is one of the heaviest flying birds in the World and is found mainly in the Indian subcontinent. They are categorized under Aves group largely found in numbers in Rajasthan.

question: Smallest plant:
explanation: The world's smallest flowering plant (0.1-0.2 mm, 0.004-0.008 diameter) is the watermeal or Wolffia Globosa. It is the smallest genus of the aquatic plants.

question: Largest flower:
explanation: World's largest blooming flower is the Rafflesia Arnoldii. This rare flower is found in the rainforests of Indonesia. It can grow upto 3 feet across and weigh up to 15 pounds.

question: Tallest animal:
explanation: Giraffes are the tallest land animal in the world with an average height of 5m (16 ft). The scientific name of Giraffes is "Giraffa Camelopardalis".

question: Expected life span of Frog:
explanation: Generally Frogs live, 3 to 18 years in the wild and on average 10 to 20 years in captivity, on the other hand common toad (10-12) years. Average life span of frog is 5 to 15 years. Therefore 10 years is the correct answer.

question: Right part of the Human Heart contains:
explanation: The adult human heart consists of two separated pumps, the right side (right atrium and ventricle) which pumps deoxygenated blood into the pulmonary circulation & the left side (left atrium & ventricle) which pumps oxygenated blood into the systemic circulation.

question: Which one of these sea creatures changes its color?
explanation: Color in seahorses is not fixed and changes based on mood, Surroundings, lighting, diet, age, varies with species and many factor.
